# Coding Standards

- Prefer explicit types over `var` except when the type is obvious.
- Use expression-bodied members, constructors, and properties where possible.
- Keep methods under ~30 lines unless readability demands otherwise.
- Prefer immutability where possible.
- Use `record` or `record struct` for immutable data models.
- Use natural keys for entities; only use GUIDs as shadow properties when necessary.
- Follow Microsoft naming conventions and project-specific patterns.
- Ensure all user-facing strings are localisable.
